subject: Project Management
title: Just-in-Time Production
description: A micro-lesson that makes Just-in-Time Production usable.

hook.text: Just-in-Time means producing exactly what's needed, exactly when needed. Zero excess.

buildup.text: Instead of stockpiling parts, Toyota arranged deliveries to arrive minutes before assembly.

discovery.text: JIT slashed inventory costs and forced quality up — defects couldn't hide in large batches.

twist.text: JIT made Toyota fragile to supply disruptions. One delayed shipment could halt the whole line.

climax.text: The tradeoff was deliberate: efficiency over resilience. It worked until global supply chains broke.

punchline.text: Zero waste until the supply chain coughs.

quiz.question: What is the main risk of Just-in-Time production?
